ucs.title: [LLM] MuddyWater (TA450/Mango Sandstorm) RMM-installer phishing chain — ScreenConnect/Atera/SimpleHelp dropped from file-share link
ucs.description: Hunts the signature MuddyWater initial-access pattern observed by Microsoft (Mango Sandstorm), Proofpoint (TA450) and Mandiant since 2022: phishing email with a link to a public file-sharing service (Onehub, Egnyte, Sync, Dropbox) that delivers a signed Remote Monitoring & Management installer (ConnectWise/ScreenConnect, AteraAgent, SimpleHelp, RemoteUtilities, SyncroLive). Catches the click→RMM-execution chain inside a 10-minute window on the same user/device.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as click_time values(Web.url) as click_url from datamodel=Web.Web where Web.url IN ("*onehub.com*","*egnyte.com*","*sync.com*","*wetransfer.com*","*dropbox.com*","*mediafire.com*","*sendcm.com*","*filetransfer.io*","*mega.nz*") by Web.src Web.user _time | rename Web.src as src Web.user as user | bin _time span=10m | append [ | tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as exec_time values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_name) as proc_name values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("ScreenConnect.ClientSetup.exe","ScreenConnect.WindowsClient.exe","ConnectWiseControl.ClientSetup.exe","AteraAgent.exe","setup_atera.exe","SimpleHelpCustomer.exe","SimpleHelpRemoteAccess.exe","rutserv.exe","rfusclient.exe","AnyDesk.exe","NinjaRMMAgent.exe","SyncroLive.Service.exe","RemoteUtilities.exe","SplashtopStreamer.exe") by host Processes.user _time | rename host as src Processes.user as user | bin _time span=10m ] | stats min(click_time) as click_time max(exec_time) as exec_time values(click_url) as click_url values(proc_name) as rmm_tool values(parent) as parent_proc values(cmdline) as cmdline by src user | where isnotnull(click_time) AND isnotnull(exec_time) AND (exec_time-click_time)<=600 AND (exec_time-click_time)>=0 | eval delay_sec=exec_time-click_time | table click_time exec_time delay_sec src user click_url rmm_tool parent_proc cmdline
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let WindowSec = 600;
let FileShareHosts = dynamic(["onehub.com","egnyte.com","sync.com","wetransfer.com","dropbox.com","mediafire.com","sendcm.com","filetransfer.io","mega.nz","4sync.com","storage.googleapis.com"]);
let RmmBinaries = dynamic(["screenconnect.clientsetup.exe","screenconnect.windowsclient.exe","connectwisecontrol.clientsetup.exe","ateraagent.exe","setup_atera.exe","simplehelpcustomer.exe","simplehelpremoteaccess.exe","rutserv.exe","rfusclient.exe","anydesk.exe","ninjarmmagent.exe","syncrolive.service.exe","remoteutilities.exe","splashtopstreamer.exe","clientsetup.exe"]);
let Clicks = UrlClickEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where ActionType in ("ClickAllowed","ClickedThrough")
| where Url has_any (FileShareHosts)
| project NetworkMessageId, ClickTime = Timestamp, ClickAcct = AccountUpn, ClickUrl = Url, ClickIP = IPAddress;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ (RmmBinaries)
   or InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (RmmBinaries)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","outlook.exe","explorer.exe","7zg.exe","7zfm.exe","winrar.exe","msiexec.exe")
   or FolderPath has_any (@"\Downloads\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Public\")
| join kind=inner Clicks on $left.AccountUpn == $right.ClickAcct
| where Timestamp between (ClickTime .. ClickTime + WindowSec * 1s)
| project ClickTime, ProcessTime = Timestamp,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, ClickTime),
          DeviceName, AccountUpn, ClickUrl, ClickIP,
          RmmBinary = FileName, FolderPath, ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentBinary = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          SHA256
| order by ProcessTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let RmmBinaries = dynamic(["screenconnect.clientsetup.exe","screenconnect.windowsclient.exe","connectwisecontrol.clientsetup.exe","ateraagent.exe","setup_atera.exe","simplehelpcustomer.exe","simplehelpremoteaccess.exe","rutserv.exe","rfusclient.exe","anydesk.exe","ninjarmmagent.exe","syncrolive.service.exe","remoteutilities.exe","splashtopstreamer.exe"]);
let FileShareHosts = dynamic(["onehub.com","egnyte.com","sync.com","wetransfer.com","dropbox.com","mediafire.com","sendcm.com","filetransfer.io","mega.nz","4sync.com"]);
let WebClicks = ImWebSession
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where Url has_any (FileShareHosts)
| project ClickTime = TimeGenerated, SrcUser = SrcUsername, SrcHost = SrcHostname, ClickUrl = Url;
ImProcessCreate
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| extend ChildBin  = tolower(tostring(split(TargetProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| extend ParentBin = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ChildBin in (RmmBinaries) or ParentBin in (RmmBinaries)
| where ParentBin in ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","outlook.exe","explorer.exe","msiexec.exe")
   or TargetProcessName has_any (@"\Downloads\", @"\Temp\", @"\Public\")
| join kind=inner WebClicks on $left.DvcHostname == $right.SrcHost
| where TimeGenerated between (ClickTime .. ClickTime + 10m)
| project ClickTime, ExecTime = TimeGenerated,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', TimeGenerated, ClickTime),
          DvcHostname, ActorUsername, ClickUrl,
          RmmBinary = ChildBin, ParentBin,
          TargetProcessCommandLine, ParentProcessCommandLine
| order by ExecTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: MuddyWater RMM Installer Spawned by Browser or Email Client
id: 7c2e1a90-9d4f-4e6a-9a3b-muddyrmm0001
status: experimental
description: Detects MuddyWater/TA450 initial access — RMM installer (ScreenConnect, Atera, SimpleHelp, RemoteUtilities, AnyDesk, NinjaRMM, SyncroLive) spawned as a child of a browser, mail client or archiver. Aligns with Microsoft Mango Sandstorm and Proofpoint TA450 reporting on RMM-via-fileshare phishing.
references:
    - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2023/04/18/mercury-and-dev-1084-destructive-attack-on-hybrid-environment/
    - https://www.proofpoint.com/us/blog/threat-insight/security-brief-ta450-pursues-payroll-themed-financial-lures
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/08
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
    - attack.t1566.002
    - attack.t1204.001
    - attack.t1219
    - attack.g0069
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: windows
detection:
    selection_child:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\ScreenConnect.ClientSetup.exe'
            - '\ScreenConnect.WindowsClient.exe'
            - '\ConnectWiseControl.ClientSetup.exe'
            - '\AteraAgent.exe'
            - '\setup_atera.exe'
            - '\SimpleHelpCustomer.exe'
            - '\SimpleHelpRemoteAccess.exe'
            - '\rutserv.exe'
            - '\rfusclient.exe'
            - '\AnyDesk.exe'
            - '\NinjaRMMAgent.exe'
            - '\SyncroLive.Service.exe'
            - '\RemoteUtilities.exe'
            - '\SplashtopStreamer.exe'
    selection_parent:
        ParentImage|endswith:
            - '\chrome.exe'
            - '\msedge.exe'
            - '\firefox.exe'
            - '\brave.exe'
            - '\outlook.exe'
            - '\explorer.exe'
            - '\7zG.exe'
            - '\WinRAR.exe'
            - '\msiexec.exe'
    selection_path:
        Image|contains:
            - '\Downloads\'
            - '\AppData\Local\Temp\'
            - '\Users\Public\'
    condition: selection_child and (selection_parent or selection_path)
falsepositives:
    - Sanctioned IT/MSP onboarding pushing ScreenConnect/Atera via the same channels — allowlist by signing cert + corporate distribution path
level: high
ucs.rationale: MuddyWater is the only major state actor whose primary initial-access pattern is signed-RMM delivery (vs. macro/loader malware). Microsoft's Mango Sandstorm reporting, Proofpoint's TA450 series and Group-IB's Earth Vetala writeups all converge on the same chain: file-sharing-link phish → user-installed RMM agent → hands-on-keyboard. Combining (a) a click on a known free file-share host, (b) a 10-m
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] MuddyWater PowGoop loader — goopdate.dll side-loaded by GoogleUpdate.exe from non-Google directory
ucs.description: Hunts the signature MuddyWater PowGoop / Small Sieve loader chain documented by CISA AA22-055A, Mandiant and DeepInstinct. PowGoop drops a legitimate signed Google Update binary (GoogleUpdate.exe / goopdate.exe) alongside a malicious goopdate.dll plus a config.txt; the DLL is side-loaded and decrypts an encoded PowerShell stage. The exact pair (GoogleUpdate.exe + goopdate.dll) executing outside C:\Program Files\Google\Update is a near-zero-FP MuddyWater fingerprint.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as first_seen max(_time) as last_seen values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.process_path) as proc_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("GoogleUpdate.exe","goopdate.exe") AND NOT (Processes.process_path IN ("*\\Program Files\\Google\\Update\\*","*\\Program Files (x86)\\Google\\Update\\*")) by host Processes.user Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=outer host [ | tstats summariesonly=true values(Filesystem.file_path) as dropped_paths values(Filesystem.file_name) as dropped_names from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name IN ("goopdate.dll","Goopdate.dll","config.txt","goopdate.dat") AND NOT (Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\Program Files\\Google\\*","*\\Program Files (x86)\\Google\\*")) by host | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` ] | where isnotnull(dropped_names) | table first_seen last_seen host user process_name proc_path cmdline parent dropped_names dropped_paths
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 30d;
let GoogleLegitPaths = dynamic([@"c:\program files\google\", @"c:\program files (x86)\google\"]);
let SuspectLoad = DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where FileName =~ "goopdate.dll"
| extend FpLower = tolower(FolderPath), IpFpLower = tolower(InitiatingProcessFolderPath)
| where not(FpLower startswith @"c:\program files\google\")
   and not(FpLower startswith @"c:\program files (x86)\google\")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("GoogleUpdate.exe","goopdate.exe","goopdateres.exe")
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
          DllPath = FolderPath, DllSHA256 = SHA256, DllSize = FileSize,
          LoaderImg = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          LoaderCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          LoaderUser = InitiatingProcessAccountName;
let Companions = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where FileName in~ ("config.txt","goopdate.dat","Goopdate.dat","goopdate.dll")
| where not(tolower(FolderPath) startswith @"c:\program files\google\")
   and not(tolower(FolderPath) startswith @"c:\program files (x86)\google\")
| project DeviceId, DropTime = Timestamp, DroppedName = FileName, DroppedPath = FolderPath,
          Dropper = InitiatingProcessFileName, DropperCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
SuspectLoad
| join kind=leftouter Companions on DeviceId
| where isnull(DropTime) or DropTime between (Timestamp - 1d .. Timestamp + 1h)
| summarize FirstLoad = min(Timestamp),
            LastLoad  = max(Timestamp),
            DroppedFiles = make_set(DroppedName, 16),
            DroppedPaths = make_set(DroppedPath, 16),
            Droppers     = make_set(Dropper, 8),
            DllHashes    = make_set(DllSHA256, 8)
            by DeviceName, DllPath, LoaderImg, LoaderUser
| order by FirstLoad desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 30d;
let GoogleLegit = dynamic([@"c:\program files\google\", @"c:\program files (x86)\google\"]);
ImFileEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where TargetFileName =~ "goopdate.dll"
| extend PathLower = tolower(TargetFilePath)
| where not(PathLower startswith @"c:\program files\google\")
   and not(PathLower startswith @"c:\program files (x86)\google\")
| project DropTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          DllPath = TargetFilePath, DllSHA256 = TargetFileSHA256,
          ActingProc = ActingProcessName, ActingCmd = ActingProcessCommandLine
| union (
    ImProcessCreate
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | extend ProcLower = tolower(TargetProcessName)
    | where ProcLower endswith "\\googleupdate.exe" or ProcLower endswith "\\goopdate.exe"
    | where not(ProcLower startswith @"c:\program files\google\")
       and not(ProcLower startswith @"c:\program files (x86)\google\")
    | project DropTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
              DllPath = TargetProcessName, DllSHA256 = SHA256,
              ActingProc = ParentProcessName, ActingCmd = TargetProcessCommandLine
  )
| summarize Events = count(),
            FirstSeen = min(DropTime), LastSeen = max(DropTime),
            Paths = make_set(DllPath, 16),
            Hashes = make_set(DllSHA256, 8),
            Loaders = make_set(ActingProc, 8),
            CmdLines = make_set(ActingCmd, 16)
            by DvcHostname, ActorUsername
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: MuddyWater PowGoop Side-Load — goopdate.dll Outside Google Update Path
id: 4f3c7b22-118a-4f4a-bf2d-muddypg0002
status: experimental
description: Detects the PowGoop loader (MuddyWater / G0069) — legitimate signed GoogleUpdate.exe side-loading a malicious goopdate.dll from a non-default directory. Pairing of the binary name + DLL outside C:\Program Files\Google\Update is a near-unique fingerprint per CISA AA22-055A and Mandiant.
references:
    - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa22-055a
    - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/telegram-malware-iranian-espionage
    - https://www.deepinstinct.com/blog/powgoop-the-evolution-of-an-iranian-loader
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/08
tags:
    - attack.defense_evasion
    - attack.t1574.002
    - attack.t1036.005
    - attack.g0069
logsource:
    category: image_load
    product: windows
detection:
    selection:
        ImageLoaded|endswith: '\goopdate.dll'
        Image|endswith:
            - '\GoogleUpdate.exe'
            - '\goopdate.exe'
    filter_legit_path:
        ImageLoaded|contains:
            - ':\Program Files\Google\'
            - ':\Program Files (x86)\Google\'
    condition: selection and not filter_legit_path
falsepositives:
    - Portable / repackaged Google Updater installers placed by a non-default deployment system (rare in enterprise — confirm signing chain and adjacent goopdate.dat/config.txt before clearing)
level: critical
ucs.rationale: PowGoop is uniquely MuddyWater — no other tracked actor reuses the GoogleUpdate.exe + goopdate.dll + config.txt triple. CISA AA22-055A, Mandiant's Iran reporting and DeepInstinct's loader teardown all anchor on this exact filename trio. Detecting goopdate.dll loaded by GoogleUpdate.exe from anywhere other than C:\Program Files\Google\Update is a near-zero-false-positive way to surface MuddyWater i
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
